text: The surgeon performs laparoscopic lysis of adhesion between the sigmoid colon and lateral pelvic wall, addressing the small bowel obstruction. The necrotic loop of ileum is resected, followed by primary anastomosis using intracorporeal technique.

The surgeon uses an energy device to perform lysis of adhesion, releasing the tight adhesion between the sigmoid colon and pelvic wall. The strangulated loop of ileum is brought into the abdomen, followed by irrigation with antibiotic solution. A small bowel resection is performed using an endo-GIA stapler, and the mesentery is ligated and divided with the energy device.

Following the resection of the small bowel using the endo-GIA stapler, the specimen is left in the pelvis for later removal. The surgeon performs a primary anastomosis within the loops of ileum using intracorporeal suturing. A stay stitch is placed and secured to the abdominal wall to aid the anastomosis. Two enterotomies are created on the proximal and distal portions of the small bowel, and the staple is inserted into both limbs to complete the anastomosis with the endo-GIA.

The surgeon inspects the anastomosis site, confirming the absence of bleeding. The opening of the anastomosis is closed using a GIA stapler and reinforced with 2-0 Ethibond sutures in a simple interrupted pattern. The mesenteric defect is closed primarily with 2-0 Ethibond sutures in a simple running fashion.
